---
title: "Background"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

## Hantavirus background table

<div class="hanta-table-wrapper">

<table class="hanta-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Virus</th>
      <th>Geographic region</th>
      <th>Human disease</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>

  <tbody>
    <tr class="row-green">
      <td colspan="3"><strong>NEW WORLD HANTAVIRUSES (Americas) causing Cardiopulmonary or pulmonary syndrome (HCPS/HPS)</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Sin Nombre virus (SNV) – main cause in U.S./Canada</td>
      <td>North America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Black Creek Canal virus</td>
      <td>North America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Bayou virus</td>
      <td>North America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Monongahela virus</td>
      <td>North America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Andes virus (ANDV) – main cause / notable for limited human-to-human transmission</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Araraquara virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Laguna Negra virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Juquitiba virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Bermejo virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Lechiguanas virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Maciel virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Orán virus</td>
      <td>South America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Choclo virus</td>
      <td>Central America</td>
      <td>HCPS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Tula virus (TULV)</td>
      <td>Europe/Asia</td>
      <td>Usually mild or subclinical infections</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>New York virus</td>
      <td>North America</td>
      <td>Associated with HPS in northeastern U.S.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr class="note-row">
      <td colspan="3">
        <strong>Definition:</strong> Cardiopulmonary syndrome affects the lungs, including pulmonary edema, and often the heart/circulation, including cardiovascular collapse. HPS is less commonly used, but appears in the literature and is generally considered analogous to HCPS.
      </td>
    </tr>

    <tr class="section-old">
      <td colspan="3"><strong>OLD WORLD HANTAVIRUSES (Europe/Asia) causing Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome (HFRS)</strong></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Hantaan virus (HTNV)</td>
      <td>East Asia (China, Korea)</td>
      <td>Severe HFRS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Dobrava-Belgrade virus (DOBV)</td>
      <td>Balkans / Eastern Europe</td>
      <td>Severe HFRS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Seoul virus (SEOV)</td>
      <td>Worldwide (rats)</td>
      <td>Moderate HFRS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Puumala virus (PUUV)</td>
      <td>Northern Europe</td>
      <td>Mild HFRS / nephropathia epidemica</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Saaremaa virus</td>
      <td>Europe</td>
      <td>Mild HFRS</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Amur virus</td>
      <td>Asia</td>
      <td>HFRS</td>
    </tr>

    <tr class="note-row">
      <td colspan="3">
        <strong>Definition:</strong> Hemorrhagic fever refers to bleeding tendencies due to vascular damage. Renal syndrome refers to kidney involvement, often leading to acute kidney injury.
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

</div>
